description: Pre-registered floors for the wiki coupling gate (tools/wiki_coupling_gate.py). These bound edge-mining PROPOSAL quality over the OKF wiki; they do NOT prove a capability. The precisionProxyFloor is the load-bearing anti-Goodhart floor: a count-only density floor is trivially gameable by emitting many single-signal edges, so the gate ALSO requires that a pre-registered fraction of proposed edges rest on >=2 independent reproducible signals, and FAILS if that proxy is below floor even when density clears.

rationale.minEdgeDensity: edges/page; conservative — a sparse corpus should still couple at least ~1 edge per 2 pages once shared-domain/tradition signals exist.
rationale.minCrossThemeCoupling: fraction of edges spanning two distinct top-level areas (area ~ domain, else pageType). Set low because most reproducible signals (shared domain/tradition) are within-area by construction; a nonzero floor guards against a purely siloed graph.
rationale.minGroundedIgnoranceCoverage: fraction of gap pseudo-nodes linked to >=1 concept; a gap that concerns nothing is ungrounded ignorance.
rationale.precisionProxyFloor: the anti-Goodhart floor — fraction of edges with >=2 independent signals. A gate that always passes is worse than no gate.
rationale.minSignalsForPrecise: an edge counts toward the precision proxy only if it has at least this many independent evidence signals.

preRegistrationProof: Floors fixed before any measured run; changing them requires a new pre-registration entry, not a tune to pass.
